title: Ensure ignore

input_row.title: Ensure ignore
input_row.description: Error policies for disks control how Input/Output (IO) errors are handled. If a read or write operation fails on the storage, an IO error occurs. Setting the error policy to recommended because it makes it difficult to determine the root cause of is not ignore any IO issues, which can lead to data loss if the data is not correctly written to the disk.
input_row.rationale: Certain applications, such as Windows Shared Cluster Filesystem, require a different behavior than the default error policy setting. IO errors need to be reported to the guest . report to errorPolicy Operating System by setting the
input_row.audit: Verify that no virtual machines are using the ignore set to errorPolicy : $ | '.items[] jq | A - ojson - vm get oc select(.spec.template.spec.domain.devices.disks[].errorPolicy == "ignore")| .metadata.name' + "/" + .metadata.namespace
input_row.remediation: Remove the report (default value) or stop or set it to errorPolicy (suggested option for Windows Shared Cluster Filesystem). errorPolicy For example, to remove the flag on the first disk (index 0) of a given VM, this command can be used: $ oc patch vm <vm-name> --type='json' -p='[ {"op": "remove", "path": "/spec/template/spec/domain/devices/disks/0/errorPolicy"}, ]' flag to stop on every disk, this (experimental) command can be errorPolicy To set an used: Internal Only - General $ o json | jq - name> - oc get vm <vm '.spec.template.spec.domain.devices.disks[] += {"errorPolicy":"stop"}' | oc apply -f -
input_row.impact: Emergency or maintenance activities may require reducing log generation by temporarily ignoring the IO errors. This may be accomplished by setting the to errorPolicy ignore .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The `audit` field provides a command indicative of a capability to programmatically verify the error policy settings of virtual machines in OpenShift.
- The operation appears to be manageable through API calls or CLI commands, as it provides specific conditions on what to check.
- Since the audit procedure outlines retrieving VM configurations and checking if any are using the `ignore` error policy, this can be automated via IBM Cloud APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_virtual_machine_error_policy_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The program would check for the error policy settings of virtual machines in a Kubernetes environment.
- Utilize the IBM Cloud CLI to run commands similar to the provided `audit` procedure:
  - Command: `oc get vm --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.template.spec.domain.devices.disks[].errorPolicy == "ignore") | .metadata.name + "/" + .metadata.namespace'`
- This command checks all virtual machines across namespaces and filters for any that have the error policy set to `ignore`.
- Specific resources to enumerate would include all virtual machine instances in the OpenShift Kubernetes cluster.
- **Pass/Fail criteria:**
  - **Pass**: No virtual machines should appear in the output of the command.
  - **Fail**: Any virtual machine name that shows up indicates non-compliance due to the use of the `ignore` setting.
- The expected output should be an empty result set (indicating that no VMs are set to ignore IO errors). If any names are returned, corrective action is needed.
